methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $205M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $600M - $3.3B.

methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 7.4x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Strand Therapeutics is a Series B-stage biotech company founded by Jake Becraft and Tasuku Kitada, having raised $205M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 8 private peers in Biotech & Health at the same stage, including Eikon Therapeutics ($3.3B), Neko Health ($1.8B), New Limit ($810M), Asimov ($800M), and Profluent ($600M), alongside 3 public biotech comparables (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L).

Two valuation methods prove applicable. Stage-Progression applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier to the $205M total raised as a proxy baseline, yielding $390M. Peer-Median anchors to the 5-company private comparable set, which spans $600M to $3.3B with a median of $810M. Public-Comp Anchoring cannot be applied due to the absence of revenue data. The public peer set (Biotech & Health median P/S of 7.4x) remains in reserve pending disclosure of target revenue.

The triangulated range across the two applicable methods spans $331M (low), $600M (median), to $931M (high). This reflects the wide dispersion within the peer set, where valuations range from $600M to $3.3B. Key uncertainties include: the target's stage of clinical/preclinical advancement relative to peer benchmarks, and the specific revenue trajectory assumptions embedded in comparable valuations. The median of $600M aligns with the lower-quartile peers but sits materially below the full peer range, suggesting valuation will depend heavily on program maturity and recent financing momentum.
